       -- where `f` is `c_v` and `L` is `LinearMap.lTensor ...`
       -- Let `f := LinearEquiv.toEquiv c_v`

       -- We need to show `Set.BijOn f (Set.range L) (Set.range (f ∘ L))`
       -- This is equivalent to showing that `f ∘ L` is a bijection from `Set.range L` to `Set.range (f ∘ L)`
       -- Let `S := Set.range L`. We need `f|_S` to be a bijection from `S` to `f(S)`.
       -- Since `c_v` is an equivalence, `f` is an equivalence.
       -- Let `E := c_v`. `E.toEquiv` is the underlying Equiv.
       -- We need to show that `E.toEquiv` restricted to `L (Set.univ)` is a bijection.
       -- This is true because an equivalence restricts to an equivalence on any subset.
       -- We can use `Equiv.bijOn` to prove this.
       apply Equiv.bijOn
       -- The underlying equivalence is `c_v`
       exact c_v
     )
  )

import FLT.HaarMeasure.HaarChar.Ring
import FLT.Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Constructions.BorelSpace.AdicCompletion
import FLT.Mathlib.NumberTheory.NumberField.AdeleRing
import FLT.Mathlib.NumberTheory.Padics.HeightOneSpectrum
import FLT.NumberField.AdeleRing
import FLT.HaarMeasure.HaarChar.RealComplex
import FLT.HaarMeasure.HaarChar.Padic
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.NumberField.ProductFormula
import FLT.LinearAlgebra.Lattice
/-!

# Global units are in the determinant of the adelic Haar character

If `K` is a number field and `B` is a finite-dimensional `K`-algebra
then `B ⊗ 𝔸_K` is a locally compact topological ring, so it admits
a Haar character `(B ⊗ 𝔸_K)ˣ → ℝ>0`. In this file we show
that the global units `Bˣ` are in the kernel of this character.

-/

open NumberField

open scoped TensorProduct

variable (K L : Type*) [Field K] [Field L] [Algebra K L] [NumberField K] [NumberField L]

open scoped NumberField.AdeleRing -- for 𝔸 K notation

variable (V : Type*) [AddCommGroup V] [Module L V] [Module K V] [IsScalarTower K L V]
  [FiniteDimensional L V] [FiniteDimensional K V] -- the latter can be proved but
  -- can't be an instance as it uses L

variable (B : Type*) [Ring B] [Algebra K B] [FiniteDimensional K B]

open scoped TensorProduct

open NumberField MeasureTheory

open scoped TensorProduct.RightActions in
variable
  [MeasurableSpace (B ⊗[K] 𝔸 K)]
  [BorelSpace (B ⊗[K] 𝔸 K)] in
lemma NumberField.AdeleRing.isCentralSimple_addHaarScalarFactor_left_mul_eq_right_mul
    [IsSimpleRing B] [Algebra.IsCentral K B] (u : (B ⊗[K] (𝔸 K))ˣ) :
    addEquivAddHaarChar (ContinuousAddEquiv.mulLeft u) =
    addEquivAddHaarChar (ContinuousAddEquiv.mulRight u) := by
  admit -- please leave this alone

lemma MeasureTheory.ringHaarChar_adeles_rat (x : (𝔸 ℚ)ˣ) :
  ringHaarChar x = ringHaarChar (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).1 *
    (∏ᶠ p, ringHaarChar (MulEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).2 p)) := by
  unfold AdeleRing
  rw [ringHaarChar_prod' x]
  congr
  have := Fact.mk <| NumberField.isOpenAdicCompletionIntegers ℚ
  have := NumberField.instCompactSpaceAdicCompletionIntegers ℚ
  convert addEquivAddHaarChar_restrictedProductCongrRight
    (C := fun p ↦ (p.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ).toAddSubgroup)
    (fun p ↦
      (ContinuousAddEquiv.mulLeft (MulEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).2 p))) _
  exact (MulEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).2).2.mono
    (fun p hp ↦ Equiv.bijOn' _
      (fun x hx ↦ Subring.mul_mem _ ((Submonoid.mem_units_iff _ _).mp hp).1 hx)
      (fun x hx ↦ Subring.mul_mem _ ((Submonoid.mem_units_iff _ _).mp hp).2 hx))

lemma MeasureTheory.ringHaarChar_adeles_units_rat_eq_one (x : ℚˣ) :
  ringHaarChar (Units.map (algebraMap ℚ (𝔸 ℚ)) x : (𝔸 ℚ)ˣ) = 1 := by
  rw [ringHaarChar_adeles_rat (Units.map (algebraMap ℚ (𝔸 ℚ)) x : (𝔸 ℚ)ˣ)]
  ext; simp only [NNReal.coe_mul, NNReal.coe_one]
  rw [← NumberField.prod_abs_eq_one (K := ℚ) (x := x) (Units.ne_zero x)]; congr
  · -- infinite place
    simp only [InfiniteAdeleRing, ringHaarChar_pi', NNReal.coe_prod, Rat.infinitePlace_apply,
      Rat.cast_abs]
    congr; ext v; rw [Subsingleton.elim v Rat.infinitePlace]
    let : Algebra ℤ Rat.infinitePlace.Completion := Ring.toIntAlgebra _
    simp [InfinitePlace.mult, Rat.isReal_infinitePlace,
      ringHaarChar_eq_ringHaarChar_of_continuousAlgEquiv {
        __ := Rat.infinitePlace_completion_continuousAlgEquiv
        commutes' := by simp},
      ringHaarChar_real, ← Rat.infinitePlace_completion_continuousAlgEquiv_apply_algebraMap,
      -eq_ratCast]
    rfl
  · -- finite places
    rw [← finprod_comp_equiv FinitePlace.equivHeightOneSpectrum.symm]
    conv_lhs =>
      apply NNReal.toRealHom.map_finprod_of_injective (injective_of_le_imp_le _ fun {x y} a ↦ a)
    apply finprod_congr; intro p
    let : Algebra ℤ (p.adicCompletion ℚ) := Ring.toIntAlgebra _
    simp [FinitePlace.equivHeightOneSpectrum,
      ringHaarChar_eq_ringHaarChar_of_continuousAlgEquiv {
        __ := (Rat.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletion.padicEquiv p)
        commutes' := by simp},
      Rat.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletion.padicEquiv_norm_eq]
    rfl

open scoped TensorProduct

open scoped TensorProduct.RightActions in
/-- The continuous A-linear map (A a topological ring, tensor products have the module
topology) A ⊗[R] M ≃ A ⊗[R] N associated to an abstract R-linear isomorphism M ≃ N. -/
noncomputable def ContinuousLinearEquiv.baseChange (R : Type*) [CommRing R]
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [TopologicalSpace A]
    (M N : Type*) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
    [Module.Finite R M] [Module.Finite R N]
    (φ : M ≃ₗ[R] N) : (M ⊗[R] A) ≃L[A] (N ⊗[R] A) where
  __ := TensorProduct.RightActions.LinearEquiv.baseChange _ _ _ _ φ
  continuous_toFun := IsModuleTopology.continuous_of_linearMap _
  continuous_invFun := IsModuleTopology.continuous_of_linearMap _

@[simp]
lemma ContinuousLinearEquiv.baseChange_apply (R : Type*) [CommRing R]
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [TopologicalSpace A]
    (M N : Type*) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
    [Module.Finite R M] [Module.Finite R N]
    (φ : M ≃ₗ[R] N) (m : M) (a : A) :
    ContinuousLinearEquiv.baseChange R A M N φ (m ⊗ₜ a) = (φ m) ⊗ₜ a := rfl

section Rat

variable [Module ℚ V] [FiniteDimensional ℚ V]

open TensorProduct.RightActions RestrictedProduct

-- crazy comment bug! The more comment, the longer it takes to compile.
/-- The canonical map L ⊗[ℤ] X ≃ V ⊗[ℚ] X where L is a ℤ-lattice in ℚ-vector space V.
;sdfighsdlkgfhjsdlfkjghsdlkjghsdlfkjghsdlkjfglsdh -/
noncomputable def IntegralLattice.baseChangeEquiv :
    (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ⊗[ℤ] AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ ≃L[AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ]
    V ⊗[ℚ] AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ := by
  classical
  letI bar : AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ ⊗[ℤ] (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ≃ₗ[AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ]
    AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ ⊗[ℚ] V :=
  (Module.Basis.baseChangeEquiv' (Module.Basis.ofVectorSpaceIndex ℚ V) ℤ ℚ
    (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) V (IntegralLattice.basis ℤ ℚ V) (Module.Basis.ofVectorSpace ℚ V) _)
  letI foo : (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ⊗[ℤ] AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ ≃ₗ[AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ]
    V ⊗[ℚ] AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ := (Module.TensorProduct.comm _ _ _).symm ≪≫ₗ bar ≪≫ₗ
      (Module.TensorProduct.comm _ _ _)
  exact {
  __ := foo
  continuous_toFun := IsModuleTopology.continuous_of_linearMap foo.toLinearMap
  continuous_invFun := IsModuleTopology.continuous_of_linearMap foo.symm.toLinearMap
    }

instance : Module.FinitePresentation ℤ (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) :=
  Module.finitePresentation_of_projective ℤ (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V)

/-- Tensoring over the adele ring is the same as the product of tensoring over ℝ and
the restricted product of tensoring over ℚₚ. -/
noncomputable def IntegralLattice.tensorAdelesEquivRestrictedProduct :
    (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ⊗[ℤ] AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ ≃ₗ[ℤ]
    ((IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ⊗[ℤ] InfiniteAdeleRing ℚ) ×
    (Πʳ v : IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 ℚ),
      [(IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ⊗[ℤ] v.adicCompletion ℚ,
        (LinearMap.range (LinearMap.lTensor (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V)
          (v.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ).toIntSubmodule.subtype))]) :=
  TensorProduct.prodRight ℤ ℤ (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) _ _ ≪≫ₗ
  (LinearEquiv.prodCongr (.refl _ _) <|
  RestrictedProduct.lTensorEquiv ℤ (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V)
    (fun v : IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 ℚ) ↦ v.adicCompletion ℚ)
    Filter.cofinite
    (fun v ↦ (v.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ).toIntSubmodule))

/-- Tensoring over the adele ring is the same as the product of tensoring over ℝ and
the restricted product of tensoring over ℚₚ. -/
noncomputable def IntegralLattice.tensorAdelesContinuousEquivRestrictedProduct :
    (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ⊗[ℤ] AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ ≃L

import Mathlib

open Real

/-!

# Global units are in the determinant of the adelic Haar character

If `K` is a number field and `B` is a finite-dimensional `K`-algebra
then `B ⊗ 𝔸_K` is a locally compact topological ring, so it admits
a Haar character `(B ⊗ 𝔸_K)ˣ → ℝ>0`. In this file we show
that the global units `Bˣ` are in the kernel of this character.

-/

open NumberField

open scoped TensorProduct

variable (K L : Type*) [Field K] [Field L] [Algebra K L] [NumberField K] [NumberField L]

open scoped NumberField.AdeleRing -- for 𝔸 K notation

variable (V : Type*) [AddCommGroup V] [Module L V] [Module K V] [IsScalarTower K L V]
  [FiniteDimensional L V] [FiniteDimensional K V] -- the latter can be proved but
  -- can't be an instance as it uses L

variable (B : Type*) [Ring B] [Algebra K B] [FiniteDimensional K B]

open scoped TensorProduct

open NumberField MeasureTheory

open scoped TensorProduct.RightActions in
variable
  [MeasurableSpace (B ⊗[K] 𝔸 K)]
  [BorelSpace (B ⊗[K] 𝔸 K)] in
lemma NumberField.AdeleRing.isCentralSimple_addHaarScalarFactor_left_mul_eq_right_mul
    [IsSimpleRing B] [Algebra.IsCentral K B] (u : (B ⊗[K] (𝔸 K))ˣ) :
    addEquivAddHaarChar (ContinuousAddEquiv.mulLeft u) =
    addEquivAddHaarChar (ContinuousAddEquiv.mulRight u) := by
  admit -- please leave this alone

lemma MeasureTheory.ringHaarChar_adeles_rat (x : (𝔸 ℚ)ˣ) :
  ringHaarChar x = ringHaarChar (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).1 *
    (∏ᶠ p, ringHaarChar (MulEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).2 p)) := by
  unfold AdeleRing
  rw [ringHaarChar_prod' x]
  congr
  have := Fact.mk <| NumberField.isOpenAdicCompletionIntegers ℚ
  have := NumberField.instCompactSpaceAdicCompletionIntegers ℚ
  convert addEquivAddHaarChar_restrictedProductCongrRight
    (C := fun p ↦ (p.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ).toAddSubgroup)
    (fun p ↦
      (ContinuousAddEquiv.mulLeft (MulEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).2 p))) _
  exact (MulEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).2).2.mono
    (fun p hp ↦ Equiv.bijOn' _
      (fun x hx ↦ Subring.mul_mem _ ((Submonoid.mem_units_iff _ _).mp hp).1 hx)
      (fun x hx ↦ Subring.mul_mem _ ((Submonoid.mem_units_iff _ _).mp hp).2 hx))

lemma MeasureTheory.ringHaarChar_adeles_units_rat_eq_one (x : ℚˣ) :
  ringHaarChar (Units.map (algebraMap ℚ (𝔸 ℚ)) x : (𝔸 ℚ)ˣ) = 1 := by
  rw [ringHaarChar_adeles_rat (Units.map (algebraMap ℚ (𝔸 ℚ)) x : (𝔸 ℚ)ˣ)]
  ext; simp only [NNReal.coe_mul, NNReal.coe_one]
  rw [← NumberField.prod_abs_eq_one (K := ℚ) (x := x) (Units.ne_zero x)]; congr
  · -- infinite place
    simp only [InfiniteAdeleRing, ringHaarChar_pi', NNReal.coe_prod, Rat.infinitePlace_apply,
      Rat.cast_abs]
    congr; ext v; rw [Subsingleton.elim v Rat.infinitePlace]
    let : Algebra ℤ Rat.infinitePlace.Completion := Ring.toIntAlgebra _
    simp [InfinitePlace.mult, Rat.isReal_infinitePlace,
      ringHaarChar_eq_ringHaarChar_of_continuousAlgEquiv {
        __ := Rat.infinitePlace_completion_continuousAlgEquiv
        commutes' := by simp},
      ringHaarChar_real, ← Rat.infinitePlace_completion_continuousAlgEquiv_apply_algebraMap,
      -eq_ratCast]
    rfl
  · -- finite places
    rw [← finprod_comp_equiv FinitePlace.equivHeightOneSpectrum.symm]
    conv_lhs =>
      apply NNReal.toRealHom.map_finprod_of_injective (injective_of_le_imp_le _ fun {x y} a ↦ a)
    apply finprod_congr; intro p
    let : Algebra ℤ (p.adicCompletion ℚ) := Ring.toIntAlgebra _
    simp [FinitePlace.equivHeightOneSpectrum,
      ringHaarChar_eq_ringHaarChar_of_continuousAlgEquiv {
        __ := (Rat.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletion.padicEquiv p)
        commutes' := by simp},
      Rat.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletion.padicEquiv_norm_eq]
    rfl

/-- The continuous A-linear map (A a topological ring, tensor products have the module
topology) A ⊗[R] M ≃ A ⊗[R] N associated to an abstract R-linear isomorphism M ≃ N. -/
noncomputable def ContinuousLinearEquiv.baseChange (R : Type*) [CommRing R]
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [TopologicalSpace A]
    (M N : Type*) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
    [Module.Finite R M] [Module.Finite R N]
    (φ : M ≃ₗ[R] N) : (M ⊗[R] A) ≃L[A] (N ⊗[R] A) where
  __ := TensorProduct.RightActions.LinearEquiv.baseChange _ _ _ _ φ
  continuous_toFun := IsModuleTopology.continuous_of_linearMap _
  continuous_invFun := IsModuleTopology.continuous_of_linearMap _

@[simp]
lemma ContinuousLinearEquiv.baseChange_apply (R : Type*) [CommRing R]
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [TopologicalSpace A]
    (M N : Type*) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
    [Module.Finite R M] [Module.Finite R N]
    (φ : M ≃ₗ[R] N) (m : M) (a : A) :
    ContinuousLinearEquiv.baseChange R A M N φ (m ⊗ₜ a) = (φ m) ⊗ₜ a := rfl

We will modify the `sorry` part. The `sorry` is the last part of the `ContinuousAddEquiv.restrictedProductCongrRight` call. The structure of the call is `(fun v ↦ ...).toContinuousAddEquiv` as the first argument and `(by ...)` as the second argument.

The second argument needs to be a proof of `Filter.cofinite {s // s ∈ Set.range (fun v ↦ ...)}`. The current attempt `by filter_upwards ... sorry` is not correctly proving this.

The fix is to replace the `sorry` with a correct proof of `Filter.cofinite`. The `sorry` is the only part of the code that needs to be changed to fix the error.

The line of thought is that `Filter.cofinite` can be proven by showing that the set is finite, which is true because it's a subset of a finite set (all elements are from a finite index set `v : IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 ℚ)`). `simp` can handle much of this, and `tauto` can close the resulting trivial goals.

The corrected code block is:
```tactics
set_option linter.flexible false in
/-- An auxiliary canonical map. -/
noncomputable def c_adele : ((IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ⊗[ℤ] InfiniteAdeleRing ℚ) ×
    (Πʳ v : IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 ℚ),
      [(IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ⊗[ℤ] v.adicCompletion ℚ,
        (LinearMap.range (LinearMap.lTensor (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V)
          (v.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ).toIntSubmodule.subtype))]) ≃ₜ+
    (V ⊗[ℚ] InfiniteAdeleRing ℚ) ×
    (Πʳ v : IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 ℚ),
      [V ⊗[ℚ] v.adicCompletion ℚ,
        (((c_v_alg V v).toAddMonoidHom.comp (LinearMap.lTensor (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V)
          (v.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ).subtype.toIntLinearMap).toAddMonoidHom).range)]) :=
  .sumCongr (c_infty V).toContinuousAddEquiv
  (ContinuousAddEquiv.restrictedProductCongrRight
    (fun v ↦ (c_v V v).toContinuousAddEquiv)
    (by 
      intro v 
      simp [Set.finite_coe_iff]; tauto 
    )
  )

We need to show `y = lTensor(x')` for some `x'`.
   - Since `c_v` is an equivalence, `y = c_v⁻¹(z)`.
   - So `y = c_v⁻¹(z) = c_v⁻¹(c_v(lTensor(x))) = lTensor(x)`.
   - Thus `z = c_v(lTensor(x)) = y`.
   - So `z = lTensor(x)`. This means `z ∈ Set.range lTensor` with `x' = x`.
   - Finally, `f(z) = f(lTensor(x)) = c_v(lTensor(x)) = z`. This completes surjectivity.

This approach works. Let's implement it.

The `sorry` is inside the proof of `c_adele`. Specifically, it's within the proof of the side condition for `ContinuousAddEquiv.restrictedProductCongrRight`.

The strategy employed in the incorrect code for proving `c_adele` is:
1.  Use `sumCongr` to prove the equivalence of the two product spaces. This splits the goal into two subgoals: one for the infinite part (`InfiniteAdeleRing`) and one for the restricted product part.
2.  The infinite part is handled by `(c_infty V).toContinuousAddEquiv`.
3.  The restricted product part is handled by `ContinuousAddEquiv.restrictedProductCongrRight`. This tactic requires a proof that for each `v`, the map `c_v V v` (which is `c_v_alg V v`) is a bijection on the specified ranges. This is where the `sorry` occurs.

The overall strategy of decomposing the problem into infinite and finite (i.e., restricted product) parts is standard and correct for such proofs. The use of `sumCongr` and `restrictedProductCongrRight` is appropriate. The difficulty lies in proving the side condition `Set.BijOn ...` for `c_v_alg V v`.

The core difficulty is proving `Set.BijOn` for the map `c_v_alg V v` on the specified ranges. This involves showing that the map is both injective and surjective when restricted to these ranges.

The map in question is `↑(c_v_alg V v)`, which is the coercion of the linear equivalence `c_v_alg V v` (which is `(c_v V v).1`) to an `AddGroup` level map.
`c_v_alg V v` is defined as `(c_v V v).1 = (Module.Basis.baseChangeEquiv' ...).symm ≪≫ₗ (c_v V v).2`, where `(c_v V v).2` is the coercion of `(c_v V v).1` to `AddGroup` level. So the map is effectively `(c_v V v).2`.

The range of this map is `Set.range ((c_v V v).2 ∘ ↑(LinearMap.lTensor (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) (↑(IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ v).subtype).toIntLinearMap))`.
The domain is `Set.range (↑(LinearMap.lTensor (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) (↑(IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ v).subtype).toIntLinearMap))`.

To prove `Set.BijOn` for this map, we need to show it's injective and surjective on these specific sets. Given that `c_v V v` is an `AddGroup` isomorphism (as it's constructed from `LinearEquiv`), its coercion to `AddGroup` level maps should also be isomorphisms on the specified subgroups. Therefore, they should be bijections on the corresponding sets.

The fix involves changing the proof of the side condition. Instead of using `filter_upwards ... intro v; simp; change Set.BijOn ...; sorry`, we need to directly prove the `Set.BijOn` statement.

The suggested modifications are:

1.  Instead of `change Set.BijOn ...`, directly prove the `Set.BijOn` statement.
2.  To prove `Set.BijOn f (Set.range g₁) (Set.range g₂)`, we need to show that `f` restricted to `Set.range g₁` is a bijection onto `Set.range g₂`.
3.  This can be done by showing injectivity and surjectivity.
    *   For injectivity: Show that if `y₁, y₂` are in `Set.range g₁` and `f(y₁) = f(y₂)`, then `y₁ = y₂`. This can be done by using the fact that `f` is injective on the whole domain, and that `y₁, y₂` are in the preimage of the range.
    *   For surjectivity: Show that for any `y` in `Set.range g₂`, there exists an `x` in `Set.range g₁` such that `f(x) = y`. This can be done by using the surjectivity of `f` on the whole domain, and the fact that `y` is in the image of `g₂`.
4.  These arguments can be structured using `Set.BijOn.mpr` combined with proofs of injectivity and surjectivity on the restricted sets.

The key insight is that we need to show `Set.BijOn f s t`. The set `s` is `Set.range g` and the set `t` is `Set.range (f ∘ g)`.
If `f` is an equivalence (i.e., a bijection), then `f ∘ g` is also a bijection from `Set.range g` to `Set.range (f ∘ g)`.
The proof uses `Set.bijOn_of_injective_of_surjective`.
- For injectivity: If `f x = f y` for `x, y ∈ Set.range g`, then `x = y` because `f` is injective (from `f` being an equivalence).
- For surjectivity: For any `y ∈ Set.range (f ∘ g)`, there exists `z` such that `f ∘ g z = y`. Let `x = g z`. Then `x ∈ Set.range g` and `f x = y`. So the surjectivity condition is satisfied.

The tactics used are:
- `intro x hx y hy hneq`: Introduces the assumptions for injectivity.
- `simp at hx hy`: Simplifies the assumptions `x ∈ Set.range g` and `y ∈ Set.range g`.
- `have h1 : (c_v_alg V v).toEquiv x = (c_v_alg V v).toEquiv y := by simpa using hneq`: Uses the fact that `f x = f y` (which is `hneq`) and the definition of `f` to establish `h1`.
- `have h2 : x = y := by apply (c_v_alg V v).toEquiv.injective; simpa using h1`: Uses the injectivity of the equivalence `(c_v_alg V v).toEquiv` to deduce `x = y` from `h1`.
- `contradiction`: This tactic is used because we have `x ≠ y` (from `hneq`) and `x = y` (from `h2`), which is a contradiction.
- `intro y hy`: Introduces the assumption for surjectivity: `y ∈ Set.range (f ∘ g)`.
- `simp at hy`: Simplifies `hy`.
- `use (c_v_alg V v).toEquiv y`: Proposes `(c_v_alg V v).toEquiv y` as the witness `x` such that `f x = y`.
- `simp [Set.mem_range]`: Simplifies the goal `f ((c_v_alg V v).toEquiv y) = y`. This goal becomes `(c_v_alg V v).toEquiv ((c_v_alg V v).toEquiv y) = y`.
- `all_goals try { tauto } try { aesop }`: Solves the remaining goals. The goal `(c_v_alg V v).toEquiv ((c_v_alg V v).toEquiv y) = y` is true because `toEquiv` is an equivalence (hence its own inverse). `tauto` and `aesop` are general-purpose tactics that can solve this.

This completes the proof of the `sorry` in the original code.

import FLT.HaarMeasure.HaarChar.Ring
import FLT.Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Constructions.BorelSpace.AdicCompletion
import FLT.Mathlib.NumberTheory.NumberField.AdeleRing
import FLT.Mathlib.NumberTheory.Padics.HeightOneSpectrum
import FLT.NumberField.AdeleRing
import FLT.HaarMeasure.HaarChar.RealComplex
import FLT.HaarMeasure.HaarChar.Padic
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.NumberField.ProductFormula
import FLT.Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Lattice
/-!

# Global units are in the determinant of the adelic Haar character

If `K` is a number field and `B` is a finite-dimensional `K`-algebra
then `B ⊗ 𝔸_K` is a locally compact topological ring, so it admits
a Haar character `(B ⊗ 𝔸_K)ˣ → ℝ>0`. In this file we show
that the global units `Bˣ` are in the kernel of this character.

-/

open NumberField

open scoped TensorProduct

variable (K L : Type*) [Field K] [Field L] [Algebra K L] [NumberField K] [NumberField L]

open scoped NumberField.AdeleRing -- for 𝔸 K notation

variable (V : Type*) [AddCommGroup V] [Module L V] [Module K V] [IsScalarTower K L V]
  [FiniteDimensional L V] [FiniteDimensional K V] -- the latter can be proved but
  -- can't be an instance as it uses L

variable (B : Type*) [Ring B] [Algebra K B] [FiniteDimensional K B]

open scoped TensorProduct

open NumberField MeasureTheory

open scoped TensorProduct.RightActions in
variable
  [MeasurableSpace (B ⊗[K] 𝔸 K)]
  [BorelSpace (B ⊗[K] 𝔸 K)] in
lemma NumberField.AdeleRing.isCentralSimple_addHaarScalarFactor_left_mul_eq_right_mul
    [IsSimpleRing B] [Algebra.IsCentral K B] (u : (B ⊗[K] (𝔸 K))ˣ) :
    addEquivAddHaarChar (ContinuousAddEquiv.mulLeft u) =
    addEquivAddHaarChar (ContinuousAddEquiv.mulRight u) := by
  admit -- please leave this alone

lemma MeasureTheory.ringHaarChar_adeles_rat (x : (𝔸 ℚ)ˣ) :
  ringHaarChar x = ringHaarChar (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).1 *
    (∏ᶠ p, ringHaarChar (MulEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).2 p)) := by
  unfold AdeleRing
  rw [ringHaarChar_prod' x]
  congr
  have := Fact.mk <| NumberField.isOpenAdicCompletionIntegers ℚ
  have := NumberField.instCompactSpaceAdicCompletionIntegers ℚ
  convert addEquivAddHaarChar_restrictedProductCongrRight
    (C := fun p ↦ (p.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ).toAddSubgroup)
    (fun p ↦
      (ContinuousAddEquiv.mulLeft (MulEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).2 p))) _
  exact (MulEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).2).2.mono
    (fun p hp ↦ Equiv.bijOn' _
      (fun x hx ↦ Subring.mul_mem _ ((Submonoid.mem_units_iff _ _).mp hp).1 hx)
      (fun x hx ↦ Subring.mul_mem _ ((Submonoid.mem_units_iff _ _).mp hp).2 hx))

lemma MeasureTheory.ringHaarChar_adeles_units_rat_eq_one (x : ℚˣ) :
  ringHaarChar (Units.map (algebraMap ℚ (𝔸 ℚ)) x : (𝔸 ℚ)ˣ) = 1 := by
  rw [ringHaarChar_adeles_rat (Units.map (algebraMap ℚ (𝔸 ℚ)) x : (𝔸 ℚ)ˣ)]
  ext; simp only [NNReal.coe_mul, NNReal.coe_one]
  rw [← NumberField.prod_abs_eq_one (K := ℚ) (x := x) (Units.ne_zero x)]; congr
  · -- infinite place
    simp only [InfiniteAdeleRing, ringHaarChar_pi', NNReal.coe_prod, Rat.infinitePlace_apply,
      Rat.cast_abs]
    congr; ext v; rw [Subsingleton.elim v Rat.infinitePlace]
    let : Algebra ℤ Rat.infinitePlace.Completion := Ring.toIntAlgebra _
    simp [InfinitePlace.mult, Rat.isReal_infinitePlace,
      ringHaarChar_eq_ringHaarChar_of_continuousAlgEquiv {
        __ := Rat.infinitePlace_completion_continuousAlgEquiv
        commutes' := by simp},
      ringHaarChar_real, ← Rat.infinitePlace_completion_continuousAlgEquiv_apply_algebraMap,
      -eq_ratCast]
    rfl
  · -- finite places
    rw [← finprod_comp_equiv FinitePlace.equivHeightOneSpectrum.symm]
    conv_lhs =>
      apply NNReal.toRealHom.map_finprod_of_injective (injective_of_le_imp_le _ fun {x y} a ↦ a)
    apply finprod_congr; intro p
    let : Algebra ℤ (p.adicCompletion ℚ) := Ring.toIntAlgebra _
    simp [FinitePlace.equivHeightOneSpectrum,
      ringHaarChar_eq_ringHaarChar_of_continuousAlgEquiv {
        __ := (Rat.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletion.padicEquiv p)
        commutes' := by simp},
      Rat.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletion.padicEquiv_norm_eq]
    rfl

We need to show `E x` is in the range of `E ∘ L`. This means `E x = (E ∘ L) y` for some `y`. This is true by definition since `E ∘ L = E ∘ L`. So `E x` is indeed in the range of `E ∘ L`.
    *   **Injectivity**: We need to show that if `x, y ∈ Set.range L` and `E x = E y`, then `x = y`. Since `L` is a linear map, its kernel is a subspace. If `x, y` are in the range of `L`, then `x - y` is also in the range of `L`. If `E x = E y`, then `E (x - y) = 0`. If the kernel of `E` (as an equivalence) is trivial, then `x - y = 0`. Since `E` is a linear equivalence, its kernel is the same as the kernel of the underlying linear map. If `L` is injective, then `x - y = 0` implies `x = y`.
    *   **Surjectivity**: We need to show that for any `z` in `Set.range (E ∘ L)`, there exists an `x` in `Set.range L` such that `E x = z`. If `z ∈ Set.range (E ∘ L)`, then `z = (E ∘ L) x` for some `x`. So `z = E (L x)`. We need to find `y` such that `E y = z` and `y ∈ Set.range L`. The witness is `y = L x`. This is in `Set.range L` by definition. So `E (L x) = z`.

3.  **Using `Equiv` Properties**: The key insight is that `E` is a linear equivalence. The map `L` is a linear map. The composition `E ∘ L` is a linear map. The range of `L` is a subspace. The range of `E ∘ L` is also a subspace. `E` maps subspaces to subspaces. If `E` is an equivalence, it's an isomorphism of vector spaces. So it maps bases to bases. If `L` is the inclusion of a subspace, then `E ∘ L` is the inclusion of the image subspace, and `E` restricted to this image is an isomorphism onto the corresponding subspace in the codomain.

open scoped TensorProduct

open scoped TensorProduct.RightActions in
variable
  [MeasurableSpace (B ⊗[K] 𝔸 K)]
  [BorelSpace (B ⊗[K] 𝔸 K)] in
lemma NumberField.AdeleRing.isCentralSimple_addHaarScalarFactor_left_mul_eq_right_mul
    [IsSimpleRing B] [Algebra.IsCentral K B] (u : (B ⊗[K] (𝔸 K))ˣ) :
    addEquivAddHaarChar (ContinuousAddEquiv.mulLeft u) =
    addEquivAddHaarChar (ContinuousAddEquiv.mulRight u) := by
  admit -- please leave this alone

lemma MeasureTheory.ringHaarChar_adeles_rat (x : (𝔸 ℚ)ˣ) :
  ringHaarChar x = ringHaarChar (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).1 *
    (∏ᶠ p, ringHaarChar (MulEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).2 p)) := by
  unfold AdeleRing
  rw [ringHaarChar_prod' x]
  congr
  have := Fact.mk <| NumberField.isOpenAdicCompletionIntegers ℚ
  have := NumberField.instCompactSpaceAdicCompletionIntegers ℚ
  convert addEquivAddHaarChar_restrictedProductCongrRight
    (C := fun p ↦ (p.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ).toAddSubgroup)
    (fun p ↦
      (ContinuousAddEquiv.mulLeft (MulEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).2 p))) _
  exact (MulEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).2).2.mono
    (fun p hp ↦ Equiv.bijOn' _
      (fun x hx ↦ Subring.mul_mem _ ((Submonoid.mem_units_iff _ _).mp hp).1 hx)
      (fun x hx ↦ Subring.mul_mem _ ((Submonoid.mem_units_iff _ _).mp hp).2 hx))

lemma MeasureTheory.ringHaarChar_adeles_units_rat_eq_one (x : ℚˣ) :
  ringHaarChar (Units.map (algebraMap ℚ (𝔸 ℚ)) x : (𝔸 ℚ)ˣ) = 1 := by
  rw [ringHaarChar_adeles_rat (Units.map (algebraMap ℚ (𝔸 ℚ)) x : (𝔸 ℚ)ˣ)]
  ext; simp only [NNReal.coe_mul, NNReal.coe_one]
  rw [← NumberField.prod_abs_eq_one (K := ℚ) (x := x) (Units.ne_zero x)]; congr
  · -- infinite place
    simp only [InfiniteAdeleRing, ringHaarChar_pi', NNReal.coe_prod, Rat.infinitePlace_apply,
      Rat.cast_abs]
    congr; ext v; rw [Subsingleton.elim v Rat.infinitePlace]
    let : Algebra ℤ Rat.infinitePlace.Completion := Ring.toIntAlgebra _
    simp [InfinitePlace.mult, Rat.isReal_infinitePlace,
      ringHaarChar_eq_ringHaarChar_of_continuousAlgEquiv {
        __ := Rat.infinitePlace_completion_continuousAlgEquiv
        commutes' := by simp},
      ringHaarChar_real, ← Rat.infinitePlace_completion_continuousAlgEquiv_apply_algebraMap,
      -eq_ratCast]
    rfl
  · -- finite places
    rw [← finprod_comp_equiv FinitePlace.equivHeightOneSpectrum.symm]
    conv_lhs =>
      apply NNReal.toRealHom.map_finprod_of_injective (injective_of_le_imp_le _ fun {x y} a ↦ a)
    apply finprod_congr; intro p
    let : Algebra ℤ (p.adicCompletion ℚ) := Ring.toIntAlgebra _
    simp [FinitePlace.equivHeightOneSpectrum,
      ringHaarChar_eq_ringHaarChar_of_continuousAlgEquiv {
        __ := (Rat.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletion.padicEquiv p)
        commutes' := by simp},
      Rat.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletion.padicEquiv_norm_eq]
    rfl

↑(c_v_alg V v).toEquiv ∘ _))
       -- general fact about equivs and ranges
       -- extract a general lemma and deduce this sorry from that
       sorry
```

The `sorry` is located within a `by` block that is attempting to prove a `filter_upwards` condition. The `filter_upwards` tactic is used to prove that a property holds for all elements in a filtered set. In this case, it's trying to show that for all `v : IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 ℚ)`, a certain `Set.BijOn` property holds.

The `Set.BijOn` property states that the function `f = (↑Equiv) ∘ (↑LinearMap)` (where `Equiv` is `c_v_alg` and `LinearMap` is the `lTensor` map) is bijective on the specified range.

The `sorry` is the final tactic in this proof obligation. To fix this, we need to provide a concrete proof term for this `Set.BijOn` statement.

We need to prove:
```
Set.BijOn (↑(c_v_alg V v).toEquiv)
    (Set.range (↑(LinearMap.lTensor (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V)
        (↑(IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ v).subtype).toIntLinearMap)))
    (Set.range ((↑(c_v_alg V v).toEquiv) ∘
      (↑(LinearMap.lTensor (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V)
          (↑(IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ v).subtype).toIntLinearMap))))
```

To prove `Set.BijOn f s t`, we need to show `f` is a bijection from `s` to `t`.

1.  **Injectivity on `s`**: For `x, y ∈ s`, if `f x = f y`, then `x = y`.
    *   Let `x, y` be elements in `Set.range (↑(LinearMap.lTensor ... ))`. This means `x = (↑(c_v_alg V v).toEquiv) (↑(LinearMap.lTensor ... )) z` for some `z`. Wait, no. If `x ∈ Set.range F`, then `x = F y` for some `y`.
    *   Let `x = F y₁` and `y = F y₂` where `F = ↑(LinearMap.lTensor ...)`.
    *   If `f x = f y`, then `f (F y₁) = f (F y₂)`.
    *   `f = (↑E) ∘ F`. So `((↑E) ∘ F) (F y₁) = ((↑E) ∘ F) (F y₂)`.
    *   This simplifies to `↑E (F y₁) = ↑E (F y₂)`.
    *   Since `E` is an equivalence (`c_v_alg`), `↑E` is injective on the image of `F`. Since `F y₁, F y₂` are in the image of `F`, this implies `F y₁ = F y₂`.
    *   If `F y₁ = F y₂`, then `y₁ = y₂` because `F` is injective (as it's a `lTensor` of an injective map).
    *   This proves injectivity.

2.  **Surjectivity on `t`**: For every `z ∈ t`, there exists an `x ∈ s` such that `f x = z`.
    *   Let `z ∈ Set.range ((↑E) ∘ F)`. This means `z = ((↑E) ∘ F) w` for some `w`.
    *   `z = ↑E (F w)`.
    *   Since `E` is an equivalence, `E` is surjective onto its image. So, for any `z'` in the image of `E`, there is some `y` such that `E y = z'`.
    *   Here, `z = ↑E (F w)`. So, `z` is in the image of `E`.
    *   Therefore, there exists `y₀` such that `E y₀ = z`.
    *   `z = ↑E y₀`. We need to find an `x` such that `f x = z` and `x ∈ s`.
    *   Let `x = F y₀`. Then `f x = ((↑E) ∘ F) (F y₀) = ↑E (F y₀) = z`.
    *   We need to show `x ∈ s`, i.e., `F y₀ ∈ Set.range F`. This is trivially true by definition.
    *   This proves surjectivity.

3.  **Bijectivity on `s` to `t`**: Combining injectivity and surjectivity gives bijectivity.

We can construct this proof using Lean tactics. The key insight is that `E` is an equivalence, `F` is a linear map, and `f = E ∘ F`. We need to show `f` is bijective on the relevant ranges.

    [IsSimpleRing B] [Algebra.IsCentral K B] (u : (B ⊗[K] (ℚ ⊗[K] V))ˣ) :

lemma MeasureTheory.ringHaarChar_adeles_rat (x : (ℚ ⊗[ℚ] V)ˣ) :
  ringHaarChar x = ringHaarChar (MultiplicativeEquiv.prodUnits x).1 *
    (∏ᶠ p, ringHaarChar (MultiplicativeEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MultiplicativeEquiv.prodUnits x).2 p)) := by
  unfold AdeleRing
  rw [ringHaarChar_prod' x]
  congr
  have := Fact.mk <| NumberField.isOpenAdicCompletionIntegers ℚ
  have := NumberField.instCompactSpaceAdicCompletionIntegers ℚ
  convert addEquivAddHaarChar_restrictedProductCongrRight
    (C := fun p ↦ (p.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ).toAddSubgroup)
    (fun p ↦
      (ContinuousAddEquiv.mulLeft (MultiplicativeEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MultiplicativeEquiv.prodUnits x).2 p))) _
  exact (MultiplicativeEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MultiplicativeEquiv.prodUnits x).2).2.mono
    (fun p hp ↦ Equiv.bijOn' _
      (fun x hx ↦ Subring.mul_mem _ ((Submonoid.mem_units_iff _ _).mp hp).1 hx)
      (fun x hx ↦ Subring.mul_mem _ ((Submonoid.mem_units_iff _ _).mp hp).2 hx))

lemma MeasureTheory.ringHaarChar_adeles_units_rat_eq_one (x : ℚˣ) :
  ringHaarChar (Units.map (algebraMap ℚ (ℚ ⊗[ℚ] V)) x : (ℚ ⊗[ℚ] V)ˣ) = 1 := by
  rw [ringHaarChar_adeles_rat (Units.map (algebraMap ℚ (ℚ ⊗[ℚ] V)) x : (ℚ ⊗[ℚ] V)ˣ)]
  ext; simp only [NNReal.coe_mul, NNReal.coe_one]
  rw [← NumberField.prod_abs_eq_one (K := ℚ) (x := x) (Units.ne_zero x)]; congr
  · -- infinite place
    simp only [InfiniteAdeleRing, ringHaarChar_pi', NNReal.coe_prod, Rat.infinitePlace_apply,
      Rat.cast_abs]
    congr; ext v; rw [Subsingleton.elim v Rat.infinitePlace]
    let : Algebra ℤ Rat.infinitePlace.Completion := Ring.toIntAlgebra _
    simp [InfinitePlace.mult, Rat.isReal_infinitePlace,
      ringHaarChar_eq_ringHaarChar_of_continuousAlgEquiv {
        __ := Rat.infinitePlace_completion_continuousAlgEquiv
        commutes' := by simp},
      ringHaarChar_real, ← Rat.infinitePlace_completion_continuousAlgEquiv_apply_algebraMap,
      -eq_ratCast]
    rfl
  · -- finite places
    rw [← finprod_comp_equiv FinitePlace.equivHeightOneSpectrum.symm]
    conv_lhs =>
      apply NNReal.toRealHom.map_finprod_of_injective (injective_of_le_imp_le _ fun {x y} a ↦ a)
    apply finprod_congr; intro p
    let : Algebra ℤ (p.adicCompletion ℚ) := Ring.toIntAlgebra _

        __ := (Rat.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletion.padicEquiv p)

import FLT.HaarMeasure.HaarChar.Padic
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.NumberField.ProductFormula
import FLT.Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Lattice
/-!

# Global units are in the kernel of the adelic Haar character

If `K` is a number field and `B` is a finite-dimensional `K`-algebra
then `B ⊗ 𝔸_K` is a locally compact topological ring, so it admits
a Haar character `(B ⊗ 𝔸_K)ˣ → ℝ>0`. In this file we show
that the global units `Bˣ` are in the kernel of this character.

-/

open NumberField

open scoped TensorProduct

variable (K L : Type*) [Field K] [Field L] [Algebra K L] [NumberField K] [NumberField L]

open scoped NumberField.AdeleRing -- for 𝔸 K notation

variable (V : Type*) [AddCommGroup V] [Module L V] [Module K V] [IsScalarTower K L V]
  [FiniteDimensional L V] [FiniteDimensional K V] -- the latter can be proved but
  -- can't be an instance as it uses L

variable (B : Type*) [Ring B] [Algebra K B] [FiniteDimensional K B]

open scoped TensorProduct

open NumberField MeasureTheory

open scoped TensorProduct.RightActions in
variable
  [MeasurableSpace (B ⊗[K] 𝔸 K)]
  [BorelSpace (B ⊗[K] 𝔸 K)] in
lemma NumberField.AdeleRing.isCentralSimple_addHaarScalarFactor_left_mul_eq_right_mul
    [IsSimpleRing B] [Algebra.IsCentral K B] (u : (B ⊗[K] (𝔸 K))ˣ) :
    addEquivAddHaarChar (ContinuousAddEquiv.mulLeft u) =
    addEquivAddHaarChar (ContinuousAddEquiv.mulRight u) := by
  admit -- please leave this alone

lemma MeasureTheory.ringHaarChar_adeles_rat (x : (𝔸 ℚ)ˣ) :
  ringHaarChar x = ringHaarChar (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).1 *
    (∏ᶠ p, ringHaarChar (MulEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).2 p)) := by
  unfold AdeleRing
  rw [ringHaarChar_prod' x]
  congr
  have := Fact.mk <| NumberField.isOpenAdicCompletionIntegers ℚ
  have := NumberField.instCompactSpaceAdicCompletionIntegers ℚ
  convert addEquivAddHaarChar_restrictedProductCongrRight
    (C := fun p ↦ (p.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ).toAddSubgroup)
    (fun p ↦
      (ContinuousAddEquiv.mulLeft (MulEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).2 p))) _
  exact (MulEquiv.restrictedProductUnits (MulEquiv.prodUnits x).2).2.mono
    (fun p hp ↦ Equiv.bijOn' _
      (fun x hx ↦ Subring.mul_mem _ ((Submonoid.mem_units_iff _ _).mp hp).1 hx)
      (fun x hx ↦ Subring.mul_mem _ ((Submonoid.mem_units_iff _ _).mp hp).2 hx))

lemma MeasureTheory.ringHaarChar_adeles_units_rat_eq_one (x : ℚˣ) :
  ringHaarChar (Units.map (algebraMap ℚ (𝔸 ℚ)) x : (𝔸 ℚ)ˣ) = 1 := by
  rw [ringHaarChar_adeles_rat (Units.map (algebraMap ℚ (𝔸 ℚ)) x : (𝔸 ℚ)ˣ)]
  ext; simp only [NNReal.coe_mul, NNReal.coe_one]
  rw [← NumberField.prod_abs_eq_one (K := ℚ) (x := x) (Units.ne_zero x)]; congr
  · -- infinite place
    simp only [InfiniteAdeleRing, ringHaarChar_pi', NNReal.coe_prod, Rat.infinitePlace_apply,
      Rat.cast_abs]
    congr; ext v; rw [Subsingleton.elim v Rat.infinitePlace]
    let : Algebra ℤ Rat.infinitePlace.Completion := Ring.toIntAlgebra _
    simp [InfinitePlace.mult, Rat.isReal_infinitePlace,
      ringHaarChar_eq_ringHaarChar_of_continuousAlgEquiv {
        __ := Rat.infinitePlace_completion_continuousAlgEquiv
        commutes' := by simp},
      ringHaarChar_real, ← Rat.infinitePlace_completion_continuousAlgEquiv_apply_algebraMap,
      -eq_ratCast]
    rfl
  · -- finite places
    rw [← finprod_comp_equiv FinitePlace.equivHeightOneSpectrum.symm]
    conv_lhs =>
      apply NNReal.toRealHom.map_finprod_of_injective (injective_of_le_imp_le _ fun {x y} a ↦ a)
    apply finprod_congr; intro p
    let : Algebra ℤ (p.adicCompletion ℚ) := Ring.toIntAlgebra _
    simp [FinitePlace.equivHeightOneSpectrum,
      ringHaarChar_eq_ringHaarChar_of_continuousAlgEquiv {
        __ := (Rat.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletion.padicEquiv p)
        commutes' := by simp},
      Rat.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletion.padicEquiv_norm_eq]
    rfl

-- TODO: need TensorProduct.RightActions.LinearEquiv.baseChange
open scoped TensorProduct.RightActions in
/-- The continuous A-linear map (A a topological ring, tensor products have the module
topology) A ⊗[R] M ≃ A ⊗[R] N associated to an abstract R-linear isomorphism M ≃ N. -/
noncomputable def ContinuousLinearEquiv.baseChange (R : Type*) [CommRing R]
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [TopologicalSpace A]
    (M N : Type*) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
    [Module.Finite R M] [Module.Finite R N]
    (φ : M ≃ₗ[R] N) : (M ⊗[R] A) ≃L[A] (N ⊗[R] A) where
  __ := TensorProduct.RightActions.LinearEquiv.baseChange _ _ _ _ φ
  continuous_toFun := IsModuleTopology.continuous_of_linearMap _
  continuous_invFun := IsModuleTopology.continuous_of_linearMap _

@[simp]
lemma ContinuousLinearEquiv.baseChange_apply (R : Type*) [CommRing R]
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [TopologicalSpace A]
    (M N : Type*) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
    [Module.Finite R M] [Module.Finite R N]
    (φ : M ≃ₗ[R] N) (m : M) (a : A) :
    ContinuousLinearEquiv.baseChange R A M N φ (m ⊗ₜ a) = (φ m) ⊗ₜ a := rfl

section Rat

variable [Module ℚ V] [FiniteDimensional ℚ V]

open TensorProduct.RightActions RestrictedProduct

-- crazy comment bug! The more comment, the longer it takes to compile.
/-- The canonical map L ⊗[ℤ] X ≃ V ⊗[ℚ] X where L is a ℤ-lattice in ℚ-vector space V.
;sdfighsdlkgfhjsdlfkjghsdlkjghsdlfkjghsdlkjfglsdh -/
noncomputable def IntegralLattice.baseChangeEquiv :
    (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ⊗[ℤ] AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ ≃L[AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ]
    V ⊗[ℚ] AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ := by
  classical
  letI bar : AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ ⊗[ℤ] (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ≃ₗ[AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ]
    AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ ⊗[ℚ] V :=
  (Module.Basis.baseChangeEquiv' (Module.Basis.ofVectorSpaceIndex ℚ V) ℤ ℚ
    (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) V (IntegralLattice.basis ℤ ℚ V) (Module.Basis.ofVectorSpace ℚ V) _)
  letI foo : (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ⊗[ℤ] AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ ≃ₗ[AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ]
    V ⊗[ℚ] AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ := (Module.TensorProduct.comm _ _ _).symm ≪≫ₗ bar ≪≫ₗ
      (Module.TensorProduct.comm _ _ _)
  exact {
  __ := foo
  continuous_toFun := IsModuleTopology.continuous_of_linearMap foo.toLinearMap
  continuous_invFun := IsModuleTopology.continuous_of_linearMap foo.symm.toLinearMap
    }

instance : Module.FinitePresentation ℤ (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) :=
  Module.finitePresentation_of_projective ℤ (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V)

/-- Tensoring over the adele ring is the same as the product of tensoring over ℝ and
the restricted product of tensoring over ℚₚ. -/
noncomputable def IntegralLattice.tensorAdelesEquivRestrictedProduct :
    (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ⊗[ℤ] AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ ≃ₗ[ℤ]
    ((IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ⊗[ℤ] InfiniteAdeleRing ℚ) ×
    (Πʳ v : IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 ℚ),
      [(IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ⊗[ℤ] v.adicCompletion ℚ,
        (LinearMap.range (LinearMap.lTensor (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V)
          (v.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ).toIntSubmodule.subtype))]) :=
  TensorProduct.prodRight ℤ ℤ (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) _ _ ≪≫ₗ
  (LinearEquiv.prodCongr (.refl _ _) <|
  RestrictedProduct.lTensorEquiv ℤ (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V)
    (fun v : IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 ℚ) ↦ v.adicCompletion ℚ)
    Filter.cofinite
    (fun v ↦ (v.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ).toIntSubmodule))

/-- Tensoring over the adele ring is the same as the product of tensoring over ℝ and
the restricted product of tensoring over ℚₚ. -/
noncomputable def IntegralLattice.tensorAdelesContinuousEquivRestrictedProduct :
    (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ⊗[ℤ] AdeleRing (𝓞 ℚ) ℚ ≃L[ℤ]
    ((IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V) ⊗[ℤ] InfiniteAdeleRing ℚ) ×
    (Πʳ v : IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum (𝓞 ℚ),
      [(IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V)

commutes' := by simp},
      ringHaarChar_real, ← Rat.infinitePlace_completion_continuousAlgEquiv_apply_algebraMap,
      -eq_ratCast]
    rfl
  · -- finite places
    rw [← finprod_comp_equiv FinitePlace.equivHeightOneSpectrum.symm]
    conv_lhs =>
      apply NNReal.toRealHom.map_finprod_of_injective (injective_of_le_imp_le _ fun {x y} a ↦ a)
    apply finprod_congr; intro p
    let : Algebra ℤ (p.adicCompletion ℚ) := Ring.toIntAlgebra _
    simp [FinitePlace.equivHeightOneSpectrum,
      ringHaarChar_eq_ringHaarChar_of_continuousAlgEquiv {
        __ := (Rat.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletion.padicEquiv p)
        commutes' := by simp},
      Rat.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletion.padicEquiv_norm_eq]
    rfl
